title: Badges and Patrols
prose: Fifth year brings prefect badges for a handful of students in every house — a Head of House's letter over the summer, a small silver P pinned to a chest, and, for whoever's wearing one, the sudden, unglamorous responsibility of patrolling corridors after hours and refereeing first-year squabbles.
Whether or not the badge ever came your way, you feel the shift in the year around you regardless — a house suddenly a little more organised, a little more watched, for better or worse.

choices.label: The badge is yours, for what it's worth.
It wasn't you this year — and that's fine.
